name: Skin Lesion + Sepsis Guideline Context
summary: Patient symptoms: Patient has fever for 3 days, tachycardia, fatigue, reduced appetite, and worsening weakness. Family reports child appears less responsive today.. Imaging summary: HAM10000 classifier suggests lesion class: nv.. Top evidence-backed consideration: Sepsis.

name: Skin Lesion + Pneumonia Guideline Context
summary: Patient symptoms: Adult with persistent cough, mild fever, chest discomfort while breathing, and shortness of breath for 5 days. No known chronic lung disease.. Imaging summary: HAM10000 classifier suggests lesion class: nv.. Top evidence-backed consideration: Community-Acquired Pneumonia.

name: Text + PDF Only
summary: Patient symptoms: New-onset chest pain for 2 hours, pressure-like sensation radiating to left arm, mild sweating, and nausea.. Imaging summary: no confirmed imaging findings yet. Top evidence-backed consideration: Acute Coronary Syndrome.

name: Text Only Quick Validation
summary: Patient symptoms: Fever, sore throat, dry cough, body ache for 2 days. No severe breathlessness.. Imaging summary: no confirmed imaging findings yet. Top evidence-backed consideration: Viral Upper Respiratory Infection.

name: RSNA Chest X-ray Specialist Route
summary: Patient symptoms: Fever, productive cough, right-sided pleuritic chest pain, and breathlessness for 4 days.. Imaging summary: CXR classifier top finding: pneumonia. Top evidence-backed consideration: Community-Acquired Pneumonia.

name: ISIC Skin Specialist Route
summary: Patient symptoms: Pigmented skin lesion with recent change in color and mild itching over 2 months.. Imaging summary: HAM10000 classifier suggests lesion class: nv.. Top evidence-backed consideration: Melanoma.

name: Synthetic CAP (Report A)
summary: Patient symptoms: 52-year-old male presenting with 5 days of high-grade fever reaching 39.8C, productive cough with yellow-green sputum, right-sided pleuritic chest pain worsening on deep inspiration, SpO2 93% on room air, WBC 18400, neutrophils 85%, CRP 142, procalcitonin 1.8, sodium 131, albumin 2.9.. Imaging summary: CXR classifier top finding: pneumonia. Top evidence-backed consideration: Community-Acquired Pneumonia.

name: Synthetic DKA (Report B)
summary: Patient symptoms: 28-year-old female with Type 1 diabetes, 18 hours of nausea, vomiting, diffuse abdominal pain, progressive confusion, fruity breath, heart rate 118, blood pressure 100/60, respiratory rate 28 with deep labored breathing, glucose 487, arterial pH 7.18, bicarbonate 8, anion gap 28, ketones strongly positive, potassium 6.1, sodium 128.. Imaging summary: no confirmed imaging findings yet. Top evidence-backed consideration: Sepsis.

name: Synthetic Pulmonary Embolism (Report C)
summary: Patient symptoms: 44-year-old male 10 days post knee arthroscopy with sudden dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, mild hemoptysis, swollen tender right calf, heart rate 110, SpO2 91%, recent long-haul flight, D-dimer 4800, troponin 0.08, NT-proBNP 620, pO2 68.. Imaging summary: no confirmed imaging findings yet. Top evidence-backed consideration: Pulmonary Embolism.

name: Synthetic Routine Lipid Panel (Report D)
summary: Patient symptoms: 38-year-old female for annual preventive check, no chest pain or dyspnea, BP 118/76, total cholesterol 228, LDL 148, HDL 52, triglycerides 182, fasting glucose 98, HbA1c 5.6, TSH 2.1.. Imaging summary: no confirmed imaging findings yet. Top evidence-backed consideration: Acute Coronary Syndrome.

name: Synthetic Skin Lesion ABCDE (Report E)
summary: Patient symptoms: 55-year-old male outdoor worker with changing pigmented lesion on left forearm, grew from 4mm to 8mm over 3 months, asymmetry present, irregular scalloped border, variegated color, diameter over 6mm, rapid evolution.. Imaging summary: HAM10000 classifier suggests lesion class: nv.. Top evidence-backed consideration: Melanoma.
